summaryText: Pastor Travis Hearn opened his message by discussing how easily we copy behaviors, like accents, but struggle to copy the sacrificial love of Christ. He focused on the call to move from being 'surrounded' by Christianity to being fully 'surrendered' to Jesus. The core scripture, 1 John 3:16, defines love as laying down one's life, a principle Jesus exemplified. Pastor Travis challenged the congregation to reject a 'consumer Christianity' that seeks only to receive from God, and instead become disciples who deny themselves, take up their cross, and serve the church. He warned against being trapped by our own desires, like the monkey with its fist clenched in a coconut, and urged everyone to 'lay down' their identities, influences, and incomes at the feet of Jesus, trusting that in surrender they would find true life and freedom. The message concluded with a call to shake off the 'poisonous' things in life that hinder surrender and to commit fully to following Christ.
mainPoints: True love, as defined by Christ, is demonstrated by laying down one's life in surrender and sacrifice, not by consumeristic receiving.
Many believers are 'surrounded' by Christian culture but not 'surrendered' in heart, needing to move from being church consumers to cross-bearing disciples.
We are often trapped by our own clenched fists—holding onto desires, sins, or identities—and find freedom and life only when we 'lay it down' at Jesus's feet.

versesMentioned.text: Do not conform to the pattern of this world, but be transformed by the renewing of your mind. Then you will be able to test and approve what God’s will is—his good, pleasing and perfect will.
This is how we know what love is: Jesus Christ laid down his life for us. And we ought to lay down our lives for our brothers and sisters.

Then Jesus said to his disciples, 'Whoever wants to be my disciple must deny themselves and take up their cross and follow me. For whoever wants to save their life will lose it, but whoever loses their life for me will find it. What good will it be for someone to gain the whole world, yet forfeit their soul? Or what can anyone give in exchange for their soul?'
but each person is tempted when they are dragged away by their own evil desire and enticed.
Whoever finds their life will lose it, and whoever loses their life for my sake will find it.
Then the LORD said to him, 'What is that in your hand?' 'A staff,' he replied. The LORD said, 'Throw it on the ground.' Moses threw it on the ground and it became a snake, and he ran from it.

Paul gathered a pile of brushwood and, as he put it on the fire, a viper, driven out by the heat, fastened itself on his hand... But Paul shook the snake off into the fire and suffered no ill effects.

Monday.scripture: Romans 12:2 (NIV) - Do not conform to the pattern of this world, but be transformed by the renewing of your mind.
Monday.focus: Identify one worldly behavior or custom you tend to copy.
Monday.action: Prayerfully ask God to reveal and renew your thinking in that specific area today.
Monday.prayer: Lord, transform my mind. Help me recognize and reject the patterns of this world that I have copied, and renew my thoughts to align with Yours.

Tuesday.scripture: 1 John 3:16 (NIV) - This is how we know what love is: Jesus Christ laid down his life for us.
Tuesday.action: Perform one act of selfless service for a family member or coworker without expecting anything in return.
Tuesday.prayer: Jesus, thank you for laying down your life. Teach me what love truly is by helping me lay down my own desires to serve someone else today.

Wednesday.scripture: Matthew 16:24 (NIV) - Then Jesus said to his disciples, 'Whoever wants to be my disciple must deny themselves and take up their cross and follow me.'
Wednesday.action: Identify one way you can actively serve or build up your local church this week, and take a step to do it (e.g., sign up to serve, encourage a leader).
Wednesday.prayer: God, I don't want to just consume. Help me deny myself, take up my cross, and follow you by investing my time and energy into building your church.

Thursday.scripture: James 1:14 (NIV) - ...each person is tempted when they are dragged away by their own evil desire and enticed.
Thursday.focus: Recognizing the traps of our own desires.
Thursday.action: Honestly identify one 'clenched fist' in your life—a desire, grudge, or sin you're holding onto. Write it down as an act of acknowledgment.
Thursday.prayer: Father, I confess that I am often trapped by my own desires. Give me the courage to see clearly what I am holding onto and the strength to let it go.

Friday.scripture: Exodus 4:2-3 (NIV) - The LORD said, 'What is that in your hand?'... 'Throw it on the ground.'
Friday.action: Take a quiet moment to symbolically 'throw down' what's in your hand—your key roles, talents, or resources—and pray for God to use them for His life-giving purposes.
Friday.prayer: Lord, what's in my hand? My identity, my influence, my resources. I throw them down at your feet. Take what is dead in my grip and make it alive for your glory.

Saturday.scripture: Acts 28:5 (NIV) - But Paul shook the snake off into the fire and suffered no ill effects.
Saturday.action: Make a decisive choice to 'shake off' one negative influence or toxic thought pattern. This could mean setting a boundary, choosing different media, or speaking a truth to counter a lie.
Saturday.prayer: God, you have the power to set me free. I shake off the poisonous things that have fastened onto my life. By your Spirit, protect my heart, mind, and relationships from their effects.
